Now I see:
- Middle box (c, id=1): row 24-27, col 28-31, with blue square (id=2) inside
- Left box left part (c, id=3): row 28-31, col 16-19, with blue square (id=8) inside
- Left box right part (G, id=4): row 28-31, col 20-23, with blue square (id=9) inside - this turned gray!
- White strip (W, id=5): row 28-31, col 24
- Player (N, id=6): row 28-31, col 25-27
- Blue bar (b, id=7): row 28-31, col 28-39, with blue square (id=10) inside - it has a child now!
Interesting! When I moved LEFT towards the left box:
1. The left box split into two parts: left part (c) and right part (G)
2. The right part turned gray (G) automatically
3. A white strip appeared at col 24
4. The blue bar now has a blue square inside (id=10)
So the left box has two sections: the left part (c) with 1 blue square, and the right part (G) with 1 blue square. The right part turned gray when I got adjacent.
Now I need to press SPACE to activate the gray part, which should turn it white and follow me. Then I can move it somewhere.
Wait, but the blue bar now has a blue square inside. This is different from before. Let me think about what happened:
- Before: blue bar had no children
- After: blue bar has 1 blue square (id=10) inside
So moving adjacent to the left box caused a blue square to appear in the blue bar. This is similar to how moving adjacent to the right box caused it to turn gray.
Let me press SPACE to activate the gray part of the left box.
